front: What is the Byzantine Generals Problem?
back: A logical dilemma that illustrates how difficult it is for a distributed network of parties to agree on a single coherent strategy or truth when some of the parties are unreliable or malicious, without a central authority.

A condition of a computer system, particularly distributed computing systems, where a component may fail and there is imperfect information on whether the component has failed. It represents arbitrary, malicious behavior, not just a crash or stop failure.

front: How does the Byzantine Generals Problem relate to blockchain?
back: Blockchain technology, specifically its consensus mechanisms (like Proof-of-Work), provides a solution to this problem. It allows a decentralized network of nodes to achieve agreement (consensus) on the state of a ledger, even if some nodes are malicious or act unpredictably.

The property of a system that can resist the class of failures derived from the Byzantine Generals Problem. A BFT system can continue to operate correctly even if some of its nodes fail or act maliciously.

front: What is the core requirement for solving the Byzantine Generals Problem in a network?
back: A solution requires that loyal (non-faulty) generals agree on the same plan of action, and that a small number of traitors (faulty/malicious nodes) cannot cause the loyal generals to adopt a bad plan. This is often achieved when more than two-thirds of the participants are honest.
